metadata.title:
UNCLE SAM OPENS TICKET
OFFICE NEXT MONDAY FOR


LOTTERY, WHICH WILL
HAND OUT $4,000 ACRES OF
THE ROSEBUD RESERVATION.

pages.raw_text:
Special Correspondence to The Press


DALLAS, S. D., Oct. By
thousands and by tens Of thousands
the homesteaders are gathering
here for the land rush" to the
Rosebud Indian reservation tract.


Between next Monday, Oct. 5, and
Oct. II, some $4,000 acres of farm,
pasture and timber land will be
offered at Sc an acre. For many
days the seekers have been coming
to Dallas Trains have been
crowded. Many have scorned steam,
toiling across the plains in prairie
schooners, living the nomad life.


The nomad life must continue for
thousands after they arrive here.
This town has a population of about
5000. 1t is a matter of history that
100000 people registered at Ell
Reno, Okla, in 1901, for the lands
opened there. Four years ago
100000 registered for the Gregory
county lands, near those now
sought.


So it is that Dallas is today a
city of many times 1000 people, of
whose inhabitants are living in
tents or shacks. cooking in the
open. eating under the blue sky,
and, some of them, sleeping out of
doors.


From comparatively nearby
towns comes family after family,
fits household goods loaded on
wagons. whose weary horses crawl
to some appointed spot on the out
skirts of this city of day, where
the travellers disembark and wait
patiently.


Applications may be filed at
Dallas and at Gregory. S. D., from
Oct. to II. On Oct. 10 the lot
terry drawing will be held, to deter
mine who of the pilgrims may enter
claims, and whoa vast majority
have had their journey and their
hardships in vain.


All those who draw numbers
lower than 1901 will be notified to
appear at some future date, prob.
ably in March, 1909, when each OF
the fortunate ones will be permit
ted to enter one quarter section or
less at Sc per acre. Those who
draw numbers from 4001 to 5000
will. report later, to enter any left
over lands, at $3.50 per acre.


ton to be opened for settlement is
known as Trip county and is 50
miles long by "a miles wide. In
round numbers there will be about
5000 farms of 100 acres each.


The soil is a deep, rich black
loan, founded on a subsoil of clay
of unusual depth. 1t is very fertile,
easy to cultivate and produces
every cereal and vegetable indigen.
Gus to the climate and soil of the
middle states, and also grows a lux
urious native buffalo grass that fat.
tens cattle on the range for market.


The county Is threaded with
streams. and running springs that
provide water in abundance for all
necessary purposes. Pure we'll
water Is found in sufficient guam
titles at depth of 20 to 80 feet,
